rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical decarboxylative alkoxy-alkoxycarbonylation of alkenes with potassium oxalate monoesters and alcohols to give β-alkoxy alkanoates
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of oxalate monoester to oxalate radical, which decarboxylates to an alkoxycarbonyl radical; iodide (from NH4I) acts as a redox mediator shuttling electrons to the anode; H2 evolution at the Pt cathode closes the cell balance

key_intermediates.name: oxalate radical (RO2C-CO2•) from anodic oxidation of oxalate monoester
benzyl radical from addition to styrene (II)
acyl hypoiodide (RO-C(=O)-OI) alternative precursor to alkoxycarbonyl radical (path b)
iodide / I2 redox mediator from NH4I

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes the oxalate monoester (assisted by I-/I2 mediation) to an oxalate radical which loses CO2 to give an alkoxycarbonyl radical; this radical adds to the styrene to give a benzyl radical, which is further oxidized at the anode to a benzyl cation that is captured by the alcohol nucleophile, while protons are reduced to H2 at the Pt cathode.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely suppressed (3a, 0% yield), indicating radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: α-cyclopropylstyrene (13)
probe_reagents.observation: Mainly non-ring-opened β-ethoxy ester 14 (63%) with small amount of ring-opened product 15 (10%), indicating fast anodic oxidation of the benzyl radical to cation before ring opening

probe_reagents.name: NH4I (cyclic voltammetry control)
probe_reagents.observation: CV shows substantial current increase upon addition of NH4I, consistent with iodide acting as a redox mediator

notes: Authors favor path a (direct anodic oxidation of oxalate to oxalate radical, then decarboxylation). An alternative path b — homolysis of an acyl hypoiodide RO-C(=O)-OI formed from I2 — cannot be ruled out. NH4I enhances current and likely facilitates charge transfer as an iodide mediator. Tethered-nucleophile alkenes (9, 10) give cyclized THF/γ-butyrolactone products (11, 12), consistent with carbocation capture by the pendant OH/CO2H.
